intro: Yae Miko is a 5-star Electro Catalyst sub-DPS who deploys Sesshou Sakura turrets that deal continuous off-field Electro damage. As the Guuji of the Grand Narukami Shrine, she excels in Quicken (Aggravate) teams where her turrets consistently trigger the reaction. Her Elemental Burst delivers a massive nuke by consuming all active turrets. While her playstyle requires frequent field time to refresh turrets, Yae Miko rewards with some of the highest off-field Electro DPS in the game, especially when paired with Fischl as a battery and Dendro supports for Aggravate.
weapon_bottom_line: Kagura's Verity is her signature, stacking Elemental Skill DMG. For F2P, The Widsith R5 provides massive buff windows. Solar Pearl (BP) and Skyward Atlas are strong alternatives.

weapons.name: Kagura's Verity
weapons.notes: Signature BiS. Stacks E DMG 12%×3. Yae can maintain full stacks easily with turret placement.

weapons.name: The Widsith R5
weapons.notes: Best 4-star. Random buff: 120% ATK, 96% Elemental DMG, or 480 EM. All three benefit Yae. 30s cooldown means buff every other rotation.

weapons.name: Skyward Atlas
weapons.notes: High base ATK + Elemental DMG bonus. Passive cloud attack adds extra damage. Solid stat stick.

weapons.name: Sacrificial Fragments
weapons.notes: EM-focused option for Aggravate. Skill reset allows extra turret placements. Good F2P choice.

artifact_why: Best for off-field Yae. 70% Elemental Skill DMG bonus — her turrets deal the majority of her damage. The 2pc effect (20% Skill DMG) works even on-field.
artifact_alt: 2pc ATK% + 2pc Electro DMG (Gladiator + Thundering Fury) — easier to farm. Slightly behind Golden Troupe but competitive with good substats.

main_stats.sands_note: ATK% generally. EM sands in pure Aggravate teams with low ATK buffs.

teams.name: Aggravate
teams.desc: Premium Aggravate. Nahida provides EM buff + Deepwood shred. Fischl batteries Yae and triggers more Aggravates. Kazuha groups + Electro DMG buff. Consistent high DPS.

teams.name: Raiden Hypercarry
teams.desc: Double Electro nuke team. Raiden and Yae alternate bursts. Kazuha buffs Electro. Bennett provides ATK. Massive burst damage window.

teams.name: Tighnari Spread
teams.desc: Quickswap Spread. Tighnari and Yae both deal Spread/Aggravate damage. Fast rotations. Zhongli provides comfort. High single-target damage.

rotation_code: Nahida E+Q (EM buff) → Kazuha E+Q (Electro swirl + DMG buff) → Fischl E/Q (Oz summon) → Yae E×3 (place turrets) → Yae Q (detonate turrets) → Yae E×3 (replace turrets) → Kazuha E (refresh VV) → Repeat
rotation_key: Always place 3 turrets before Q — each turret consumed adds a lightning strike. Replace turrets immediately after burst. Yae needs field time, so rotations are longer than typical quickswap teams.
mistakes: Bursting without 3 turrets — each turret consumed adds an extra lightning strike. Without 3, you lose massive damage.
Not refreshing turrets after burst — Q destroys all turrets. Always re-place E×3 immediately after.
Building full EM like Hyperbloom trigger — Yae's Aggravate damage scales with ATK and CRIT as well. Pure EM is only for Hyperbloom teams.
Using Yae as solo Electro — without Fischl battery, Yae needs 150%+ ER and loses significant damage potential.

seo.title: Yae Miko Build Guide — Best Weapons, Artifacts, Teams | GenshinGuide
seo.description: Complete Yae Miko build guide for Genshin Impact v5.6. Best weapons (Kagura's Verity, The Widsith), Golden Troupe artifacts, Aggravate teams, rotation guide.
